petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability in athletics. No specific details about the petitioner's nationality, career, or qualifications are provided in the decision text. The petition was filed with the California Service Center (WAC 03 126 54086).
outcome_reasoning: The AAO summarily dismissed the appeal because counsel failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact, as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). On appeal, counsel merely asserted that the Director erred as a matter of law and fact and acted arbitrarily by mischaracterizing the evidence, and promised a detailed brief within 30 days. However, over six months after the appeal was dated December 10, 2004, the AAO had received nothing further. Counsel did not specifically address the reasons stated for denial and did not provide any additional evidence, requiring summary dismissal.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural issue: Counsel filed the appeal on December 10, 2004, stating only that the Director erred as a matter of law and fact and acted arbitrarily, and promised a detailed brief within 30 days. Over six months later, no further submission was received by the AAO. The appeal was summarily dismissed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) for failure to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact.
